divisions.name: 6U T-Ball
divisions.description: Introductory 6U T-Ball division focused on basic skills, safety, and fun for ages 4–6.

divisions.name: 8U Coach Pitch
divisions.description: 8U Coach Pitch division where coaches pitch to players, emphasizing fundamentals and game awareness for ages 7–8.

divisions.name: 10U Minors
divisions.description: 10U Minors division with kid pitch, full defensive positions, and introductory competitive play for ages 9–10.

divisions.name: Majors (11–12)
divisions.description: Majors (11–12) division featuring full games with leadoffs, steals, and advanced rules for players ages 11–12.

divisions.name: Juniors (13–14)
divisions.description: Juniors (13–14) division that bridges youth and high school play with full-sized fields and competitive schedules.

favorite_lists.name: Favorite Teams
favorite_lists.description: Teams you follow across standings, schedules, and team pages.

favorite_lists.name: Saved Fields
favorite_lists.description: Fields you’ve added from the Fields & Locations section for quick access.

favorite_lists.name: Saved Documents
favorite_lists.description: Bookmarked rules, regulations, and policy documents for easy reference.

fields.name: Community Park – Field 1
fields.notes: Primary game field used for Minors and Majors divisions. Closest lighted field to central Schenectady.
fields.rulesText: No metal spikes allowed. Dugouts are reserved for players and coaches only. Please clean up all trash before leaving. Warm-ups in the outfield only when another game is in progress.

fields.name: Community Park – Field 2
fields.notes: Often used for weekday evening games and clinics. Scorekeeper table is located behind home plate.
fields.rulesText: Arrive no earlier than 30 minutes before game time. Batting cages are first-come, first-served with a 15-minute limit per team. No soft toss into fences. Spectators must remain behind the fence at all times.

fields.name: Community Park – Field 3
fields.notes: Frequently impacted by wet conditions; check Weather & Cancellations for updates.
fields.rulesText: Field is closed when standing water is present in the infield. Do not rake or spread water without approval from the field coordinator. No pets are allowed inside the fenced playing area.

fields.name: North Side Rec – Field A
fields.notes: Daytime-only field used for younger divisions and weekend clinics.
fields.rulesText: Games must conclude by dusk due to lack of lights. Portable restrooms are available near the parking lot. Please keep all equipment off the walking paths.

fields.name: South Side Rec – Field B
fields.notes: Practice-only field with limited amenities. Bring your own water.
fields.rulesText: No official games may be scheduled after 5:30 PM. Portable mounds are not to be moved without permission. Pack in and pack out all trash.

fields.name: Memorial Sports Complex – Field 1
fields.notes: Tournament-capable field with bullpens and full batting cages.
fields.rulesText: Team warm-ups must stay within designated bullpen and cage areas. Only league-approved batting cage balls are allowed. Concession stand rules are posted near the third-base dugout.

fields.name: Memorial Sports Complex – Field 2
fields.notes: Secondary game field often used for 8U and 10U divisions.
fields.rulesText: Home team uses the third-base dugout unless otherwise noted. All batting practice must take place in the outfield. No soft toss into the backstop.

fields.name: Lakeside Park – Field 1
fields.notes: Scenic field near the lake; limited restroom availability.
fields.rulesText: Parking is permitted in marked spaces only. Do not enter the lake or shoreline during league events. Portable restrooms may be available during tournaments.

fields.name: Lakeside Park – Field 2
fields.notes: Smaller field often used for T-Ball and Coach Pitch.
fields.rulesText: Batters and on-deck hitters must wear helmets at all times. No hitting into fences. Keep siblings off the infield during games and practices.

fields.name: Downtown Indoor Training Center
fields.notes: Indoor turf facility used for winter workouts and spring break clinics. Street parking only.
fields.rulesText: Clean turf shoes or sneakers only; no cleats allowed. Parents must remain in the viewing area. Food is not permitted on the turf. Follow posted safety guidelines for batting cages.

fields.name: Eastside Elementary School Field
fields.notes: Shared school field used primarily for practices and occasional make-up games.
fields.rulesText: School property rules apply at all times. No alcohol, tobacco, or pets on school grounds. Do not use the field when school is in session without prior approval.

products.name: Youth League Logo Performance Tee
products.description: Moisture-wicking short-sleeve performance tee with the official league logo on the chest.

products.name: Youth League Logo Fleece Hoodie
products.description: Cozy pullover fleece hoodie with front pouch pocket and printed league logo.

products.name: Youth Button-Front Home Jersey
products.description: Lightweight button-front jersey in home white with tackle-twill league logo.

products.name: Adult League Logo Cotton Tee
products.description: Classic cotton t-shirt with the league crest, sized for parents and coaches.

products.name: Youth Flex-Fit Game Cap
products.description: Official youth-sized game cap with embroidered league logo on the front.

products.name: League Logo Cuffed Beanie
products.description: Warm knit beanie with embroidered league patch, one size fits most.

products.name: Youth Practice Pants
products.description: Durable double-knee practice pants with small league logo tag on back belt loop.

products.name: Coach Sideline Polo
products.description: Moisture-wicking sideline polo with embroidered league logo for coaches.

products.name: Youth Aluminum Practice Bat
products.description: Balanced 27–29 inch youth aluminum bat, league-legal but without logo.

products.name: Youth Batting Gloves with League Logo
products.description: Padded youth batting gloves featuring a printed league logo on the wrist strap.

products.name: Adult Catcher’s Gear Set
products.description: Full adult catcher’s set with custom mask featuring a small league decal.

products.name: Team Equipment Bag
products.description: Oversized rolling equipment bag with printed league wordmark on the side.

products.name: League Logo Sticker Pack
products.description: Sheet of assorted league logo stickers for helmets, water bottles, and notebooks.

products.name: Insulated Squeeze Water Bottle
products.description: 20 oz squeeze bottle with flip-top lid; plain design without logo.

camps.name: Spring Break 9–10U Morning Skills Clinic (8:30 AM)
camps.description: High-energy skills clinic for 9–10 year olds focusing on throwing mechanics, infield drills, and base running. Early-bird start time for families who prefer an earlier session.

camps.name: Spring Break 9–10U Skills Clinic (9:00 AM)
camps.description: Core skills clinic for 9–10U players covering hitting, fielding, and throwing fundamentals. Ideal for players preparing for the Spring 2025 Minors season.

camps.name: Spring Break 9–10U Pitching & Catching Clinic (1:00 PM)
camps.description: Afternoon clinic dedicated to pitcher and catcher fundamentals for 9–10U players, including pitch grips, receiving, and blocking drills.

camps.name: Spring Break 9–11U All-Day Skills Camp
camps.description: Three-day all-day camp for ages 9–11 combining hitting circuits, defensive stations, and live scrimmages. Players are grouped by age and skill level.

camps.name: Spring Break 7–8U Speed & Agility Clinic
camps.description: Fun-focused speed, agility, and coordination clinic tailored to 7–8U Coach Pitch players.

camps.name: Summer Power Hitting Camp (11–12U)
camps.description: Advanced power hitting camp for Majors (11–12) players focusing on bat speed, launch angle, and situational hitting.

camps.name: Indoor Winter Tune-Up Clinic (7–8U)
camps.description: Preseason indoor skills tune-up for 7–8U Coach Pitch players featuring throwing and hitting stations on turf.

camps.name: Fall Defensive Skills Clinic (9–10U)
camps.description: Defensive fundamentals clinic emphasizing footwork, glove work, and infield/outfield communication for 9–10U players.

news_articles.headline: Field 3 Closed Friday, April 18 Due to Standing Water
news_articles.summary: All practices and games scheduled for Community Park – Field 3 on Friday, April 18 are canceled.
news_articles.content:
Due to heavy overnight rain, Community Park – Field 3 has significant standing water in the infield and foul territory. In accordance with league safety and field preservation guidelines, ALL activities on Field 3 are canceled for Friday, April 18, 2025. 

Affected events include all 8U Coach Pitch, 10U Minors, and Majors games and practices scheduled after 3:00 PM. Coaches will work with the scheduler to arrange make-up dates. Fields 1 and 2 at Community Park remain OPEN at this time.

Please check the News section or your team page for additional updates.

news_articles.headline: Field 3 Evening Games Delayed on April 10
news_articles.summary: Early games are canceled and later games are delayed on Field 3 for Thursday, April 10.
news_articles.content:
Light rain has left portions of Community Park – Field 3 soft but playable with extra prep. The 5:00 PM game on Thursday, April 10, 2025 is CANCELED. The 7:15 PM game is currently ON with a 30-minute delay to allow additional drying and lining.

Coaches for the canceled game should contact the league scheduler to request a make-up slot. Families scheduled for the later game should arrive no earlier than 6:45 PM and be prepared for possible additional delays if conditions worsen.

news_articles.headline: Field 2 Lightning Delay – Monday, May 5
news_articles.summary: A lightning delay is in effect for all games on Field 2 the evening of May 5.
news_articles.content:
A lightning delay has been called for Community Park – Field 2 on Monday, May 5, 2025. All games will pause for a minimum of 30 minutes following the last observed lightning strike in the area.

Players and families should move to vehicles or designated indoor shelter. Do NOT remain in the dugouts or near metal fencing. Updates will be posted here and sent via text alerts to subscribed families.

news_articles.headline: Spring 2025 Opening Day Set for April 5
news_articles.summary: Parade of teams, first-pitch ceremony, and a full slate of games on April 5 at Community Park.
news_articles.content:
Mark your calendars! Our Spring 2025 Opening Day celebration will take place on Saturday, April 5, 2025 at Community Park. All divisions are invited to participate in the parade of teams beginning at 9:00 AM on Field 1.

Following the parade, we will host a ceremonial first pitch, team photos, and a full slate of games across Fields 1–3. Concessions will be open, and league apparel will be available at the League Shop tent.

Team managers will share arrival times and parade lineup details with their rosters.

news_articles.headline: Updated Bat Standards for 10U Minors – Spring 2025
news_articles.summary: New USA Baseball bat stamp requirements take effect for 10U Minors this spring.
news_articles.content:
Beginning with the Spring 2025 season, all 10U Minors players must use bats that comply with current USA Baseball bat standards. Bats must display the USA Baseball certification stamp and comply with the maximum barrel diameter and length-to-weight ratio limits listed in the official Bat Regulations document.

Old USSSA-only or BESR-certified bats are no longer permitted in any 10U Minors games or practices. Umpires and coaches will spot-check equipment before each game.

For full details, please review the "10U Minors Bat Regulations – Spring 2025" document in the Rules & Policies section under Equipment.

news_articles.headline: Volunteers Needed for Scorekeeping and Concessions
news_articles.summary: Help keep our league running smoothly by signing up for scorekeeper and concessions shifts.
news_articles.content:
Our league depends on family volunteers to keep games running smoothly. We are especially in need of scorekeepers for weeknight games on Fields 1 and 2, as well as concessions helpers on Saturdays.

If you are comfortable with a scorebook or willing to learn, please visit the Volunteer section of the website and filter by the "Scorekeeper" role to see available game slots. Training resources and quick-reference sheets are provided.

Thank you for helping us create a great experience for our players.

news_articles.headline: Midseason Family Picnic Scheduled for June 1
news_articles.summary: Join us for a league-wide potluck, skills contests, and home run derby at Lakeside Park.
news_articles.content:
All families are invited to our Midseason Family Picnic on Sunday, June 1, 2025 at Lakeside Park – Fields 1 and 2. We will host skills contests by age group, a home run derby for older divisions, and a community potluck.

Teams will receive sign-up links for potluck items. Limited grill items and beverages will also be available for purchase to support league fundraising.

Bring lawn chairs, blankets, and your team spirit!

news_articles.headline: Spring 2025 Registration Deadline Extended
news_articles.summary: Registration for T-Ball, 8U Coach Pitch, and 10U Minors extended to March 8.
news_articles.content:
Due to strong interest and a few remaining roster spots, the Spring 2025 registration deadline for 6U T-Ball, 8U Coach Pitch, and 10U Minors has been extended to Saturday, March 8, 2025.

Families can register online through the Registration portal or in person at Community Park on March 6 from 6:00–8:00 PM. Late registrations after March 8 may be waitlisted.

Please contact the Registrar if you have questions about division placement or age eligibility.

rule_documents.title: 10U Minors Bat Regulations – Spring 2025
rule_documents.content:
The following bat regulations apply to all 10U Minors teams for the Spring 2025 season:

1. Certification: All bats must display the USA Baseball certification mark. USSSA-only or BBCOR-only bats are NOT permitted in 10U Minors play.
2. Barrel Diameter: Maximum barrel diameter is 2 5/8 inches.
3. Length-to-Weight (Drop): Maximum allowed drop is -12 (e.g., a 30-inch bat must weigh at least 18 ounces).
4. Alterations: Bats may not be altered or rolled. Any suspected altered bat will be removed from play and reported to the division director.
5. Safety: Players must wear batting helmets while holding a bat anywhere on the field of play, including on-deck areas.

Umpires and coaches are authorized to remove illegal bats from a game. Continued use of non-compliant equipment may result in player or coach suspension under league rules.

rule_documents.title: 8U Coach Pitch Bat & Helmet Guidelines – Spring 2025
rule_documents.content:
For the 8U Coach Pitch division in Spring 2025, bats must be labeled for youth play and may carry either the USA Baseball or USSSA youth stamp. Big barrel bats up to 2 3/4 inches are permitted.

All batters and base runners must wear a batting helmet with an attached face guard or C-flap. Helmets should fit snugly and be free from cracks or excessive wear.

Coaches are encouraged to verify player equipment before games and practices.

rule_documents.title: 10U Minors Division Playing Rules – Spring 2025
rule_documents.content:
This document summarizes key playing rules for the 10U Minors division during Spring 2025, including innings limits, run caps, pitching rules, substitution guidelines, and mercy rule details.

Highlights:
- Six-inning regulation games when time permits.
- Five-run cap per half-inning for innings 1–5; open scoring in the 6th.
- Pitchers are limited to 50 pitches per day and 75 per week.
- Continuous batting order is used; free defensive substitutions within pitching limits.

Refer to the full Little League rulebook for any situations not covered here.

rule_documents.title: League Code of Conduct – All Divisions 2025
rule_documents.content:
The League Code of Conduct applies to all players, coaches, umpires, parents, and spectators for the 2025 calendar year.

Key expectations:
- Demonstrate good sportsmanship at all times.
- Respect umpires, coaches, and league officials.
- Refrain from profanity, bullying, or harassment.
- Follow the zero-tolerance policy for alcohol, tobacco, and drugs at league events.

Violations may result in warnings, ejection from games, or suspension from league activities at the discretion of the Board.

rule_documents.title: 10U Pitching Safety & Rest Requirements – 2025
rule_documents.content:
To protect developing arms, the following pitching safety and rest rules apply to 10U pitchers during the 2025 season:

- 1–20 pitches: no mandatory rest.
- 21–35 pitches: 1 calendar day of rest.
- 36–50 pitches: 2 calendar days of rest.
- 51–65 pitches: 3 calendar days of rest.

A pitcher who delivers a single pitch in an inning is charged for that appearance. Pitch counts must be recorded on the official game report and signed by both managers.

rule_documents.title: Majors (11–12) Bat Regulations – Spring 2025
rule_documents.content:
Majors (11–12) players must use bats that meet current USA Baseball or BBCOR standards. Maximum barrel diameter is 2 5/8 inches. Composite barrels are allowed if properly certified.

Bats that show excessive damage, rattling, or loose end caps may be removed from play at the umpire's discretion.

rule_documents.title: 6U T-Ball Game Guidelines – 2024
rule_documents.content:
6U T-Ball focuses on instruction and fun. No official scores or standings are kept. All players bat each inning and play in the field.

Coaches may be on the field during play for instructional purposes. Games are limited to 3 innings or 60 minutes.

rule_documents.title: League Rainout and Field Closure Policy
rule_documents.content:
This policy describes how and when fields are closed due to weather, who makes closure decisions, and how cancellations are communicated.

Field coordinators inspect fields at least 2 hours before the first scheduled game. Updates are posted to the News section, team pages, and sent via email/text alerts where available.

teams.name: Tigers
teams.notes: Primary home games scheduled on Community Park – Field 2 with occasional use of Field 3.

teams.name: Cardinals
teams.notes: Shares batting cage time with Majors Dodgers on practice nights.

teams.name: Dodgers
teams.notes: Prefers late weekday games due to coach work schedule.

teams.name: Yankees
teams.notes: Home games often scheduled on Field 3; watch for weather-related closures.

teams.name: Tigers
teams.notes: 10U Minors Tigers share practice space with 10U Lions.

teams.name: Lions
teams.notes: Known for strong infield defense; typically scheduled at Memorial Sports Complex – Field 2.

teams.name: Bears
teams.notes: Home games at Lakeside Park – Field 1 with Saturday doubleheaders when possible.

teams.name: Wolves
teams.notes: Prefers early evening weekday games due to several younger siblings on the team.

teams.name: Storm
teams.notes: Rotating home schedule between Memorial Sports Complex – Field 2 and Community Park – Field 3.

teams.name: Rangers
teams.notes: 8U Coach Pitch Rangers practice twice weekly at Lakeside Park – Field 2.

teams.name: Tigers
teams.notes: Shares coach-pitch field space with the 8U Rangers on alternating days.

teams.name: Cubs
teams.notes: Primarily scheduled at North Side Rec – Field A for weeknight games.

teams.name: Eagles
teams.notes: Uses Eastside Elementary School Field for most practices and games.

teams.name: Reds
teams.notes: 6U T-Ball team with Saturday morning games.

teams.name: Mets
teams.notes: Practices at South Side Rec – Field B; games rotate among local fields.

teams.name: Bulldogs
teams.notes: 13–14U Juniors team playing primarily at Memorial Sports Complex – Field 1.
